text:
In the heart of a quiet forest, there was a small clearing where the dawn flowers would always bloom before painting their tiny faces pink and purple. Amongst these flowers sat a wise old owl, who every evening would perch on a branch and listen to the stories of the creatures that lived within these woods.

It was on a cool autumn evening that a young rabbit named Benny came hopping up to the owl. Benny lived in a meadow full of daisies, but he often visited this peculiar old owl.

"Why do you stay here so long?" Benny asked.

The owl shrugged. "It's somewhere I've always felt quite at home."

One night, after the owl had retired into its ancient feathers, Benny found courage. "I've heard some funny stories. But would you really listen to them?"

"To what?"

"The myth. They say the owl can see all the dreams you ever have. What do you think about that?"
